RabbitMQ 使用TCP套接字处理客户端连接（如AMQP、MQTT、STOM: RabbitMQ uses TCP sockets to handle client connections (protocols such as AMQP, MQTT and STOMP). This metric shows the number of TCP socket resources currently available; if they run low, new connections may be refused.
RabbitMQ 依赖文件描述符（File Descriptors）来管理网络连: RabbitMQ relies on file descriptors to manage network connections, queues, channels and other resources. This metric shows the number of file descriptors still available on the system; if they run low, new connections may be refused or the service may become abnormal.
RabbitMQ 在内存压力下会主动阻塞消息发布者（Publisher），以避免: Under memory pressure RabbitMQ actively blocks publishers to avoid a crash caused by memory exhaustion. This metric shows how much free memory is left before the blocking mechanism is triggered.
RabbitMQ 在磁盘空间不足时会主动阻塞消息发布者（Publisher），以: When disk space runs low RabbitMQ actively blocks publishers to prevent a crash caused by a full disk. This metric shows how much free disk space is left before the blocking mechanism is triggered.
发布者阻塞阈值（剩余可用磁盘空间）: Publisher Blocking Threshold (Free Disk Space Left)
在消息队列（如 RabbitMQ）中，通道是客户端与服务器之间的轻量级虚拟连接，: In a message queue such as RabbitMQ, a channel is a lightweight virtual connection between the client and the server used to transfer messages efficiently. Multiple channels can share the same TCP connection, which reduces resource consumption and improves concurrency.
当前与消息队列系统建立的客户端连接总数（包括生产者、消费者和管理工具）。建议监控: Total number of client connections currently established with the message queue system (including producers, consumers and management tools). Suggested monitoring thresholds: alert when it suddenly drops to 0 (possible network isolation); check resource limits or a possible DDoS attack on abnormal peaks. This metric reflects the overall access pressure on the system and is one of the key metrics for capacity planning and troubleshooting.
当前已被消费者接收但尚未明确确认（ACK）的消息总数。该指标直接反映系统的消息处: Total number of messages that have been received by consumers but not yet explicitly acknowledged (ACK). This metric directly reflects the reliability of message processing and the health of consumers.
当前消息队列系统中存在的队列（或主题）总数。建议监控阈值：突增：可能因配置错误导: Total number of queues (or topics) currently present in the message queue system. Suggested monitoring thresholds: sudden increase - a misconfiguration may be creating a large number of queues automatically (check the automation policy); sudden drop - queues may have been deleted by mistake or the system metadata may be broken; continuous growth - watch resource consumption (memory, file descriptors, etc.)
当前连接到消息队列并正在发布消息的生产者数量。建议监控阈值：突降为0时告警（可能: Number of producers currently connected to the message queue and publishing messages. Suggested monitoring thresholds: alert when it drops to 0 (producers may be down); watch for resource contention on abnormal spikes. This metric reflects the stability of the write side of the system and is useful for capacity planning and anomaly detection.
成功投递给消费者的消息数量。该指标反映系统的实时吞吐能力，是衡量消费者处理效率和: Number of messages successfully delivered to consumers. This metric reflects the real-time throughput of the system and is a key indicator of consumer processing efficiency and server performance.
每秒回退给生产者的不可路由消息数: Unroutable Messages Returned to Publishers per Second
每秒手动确认投递的消息数: Messages Delivered in Manual Acknowledgment Mode per Second
每秒手动确认模式下的轮询操作数: Polling Operations in Manual Acknowledgment Mode per Second
每秒自动确认投递的消息数: Messages Delivered in Auto Acknowledgment Mode per Second
每秒自动确认模式下的轮询操作数: Polling Operations in Auto Acknowledgment Mode per Second
每秒路由到队列的消息数: Messages Routed to Queues per Second
消息队列每秒接收的新消息数量。建议监控阈值：持续低于预期值时警告（可能生产者异常: Number of new messages received by the message queue per second. Suggested monitoring thresholds: warn when it stays below the expected value (producers may be abnormal); watch the processing capacity of consumers during sudden peaks. This metric reflects the write load of the system, and abnormal fluctuations may affect the overall throughput.
示消费者在手动确认（manual acknowledgment）模式下每秒接收的: Number of messages received per second by consumers in manual acknowledgment mode. This metric reflects the efficiency of message processing by consumers; a low value may be caused by complex business logic or acknowledgment delays, so the processing logic or the acknowledgment strategy may need to be optimized.
表示 RabbitMQ 当前维护的所有客户端 TCP 连接数量。该指标直接反映系: Number of client TCP connections currently maintained by RabbitMQ. This metric directly reflects the system load and resource usage. An abnormally high value (close to the system limit) may exhaust file descriptors, increase memory pressure or degrade performance, so check the client connection pool configuration or look for connection leaks. Normally it should follow the trend of the business traffic.
表示交换机因无法匹配任何绑定队列而每秒丢弃的消息数量。此指标反映路由键配置错误或: Number of messages dropped per second by exchanges because they could not be matched to any bound queue. This metric points to a wrong routing key configuration or missing consumers, so check the bindings between exchanges and queues.
表示交换机每秒成功路由到目标队列的消息数量。此指标反映消息分发的效率，若数值低于: Number of messages successfully routed by exchanges to target queues per second. This metric reflects the efficiency of message distribution; if it is lower than the publish rate, routing keys may not match or queues may not be bound.
表示因无法路由到任何队列（且启用了 mandatory 标志）而每秒被退回给生产: Number of messages returned to publishers per second because they could not be routed to any queue (with the mandatory flag enabled). This metric points to a routing misconfiguration or missing consumers, so check the binding rules of exchanges and the error handling logic of publishers.
表示因消费者未确认（NACK或超时）而每秒重新投递到队列的消息数量。此指标反映消: Number of messages redelivered to queues per second because consumers did not acknowledge them (NACK or timeout). This metric reflects consumer processing errors or performance bottlenecks; frequent redelivery may require optimizing the consumer logic or adjusting the timeout parameters.
表示已被消费者接收但尚未收到确认（ACK/NACK）的消息数量。此指标反映消费者: Number of messages that have been received by consumers but not yet acknowledged (ACK/NACK). This metric reflects the processing efficiency of consumers; continuous growth may indicate that consumers are delayed or failing.
表示当前 RabbitMQ 中所有活跃的 AMQP 通道数量。通道是客户端与服务: Number of all active AMQP channels currently in RabbitMQ. A channel is a lightweight connection between a client and the server, and one connection can contain multiple channels. This metric reflects the current concurrent communication load; a high value may increase the resource consumption of the server, so watch it together with the number of connections to avoid performance bottlenecks or memory leaks.
表示当前 RabbitMQ 集群或节点上存在的队列总数量。此指标反映系统整体负载: Total number of queues currently present in the RabbitMQ cluster or node. This metric reflects the overall load and resource usage; a high value may increase management complexity or hurt performance, so combine it with the number of messages in queues and the number of consumers to decide whether idle queues should be merged or cleaned up.
表示服务器每秒向生产者发送的发布确认（Publisher Confirm）数量。: Number of publisher confirms sent by the server to publishers per second. This metric reflects the efficiency of message persistence or routing completion; a high confirm rate usually means that the system is performing well.
表示服务器每秒未能向生产者发送确认（Publisher Confirm）的消息数: Number of messages per second for which the server failed to send a publisher confirm. This metric points to potential problems with message persistence or routing; a persistently high value may indicate a performance bottleneck or a failure.
表示每秒在 RabbitMQ 上创建（声明）的新队列数量。此指标反映应用程序动态: Number of new queues created (declared) in RabbitMQ per second. This metric reflects how actively the application manages queues dynamically; a high value may increase resource overhead because of frequent queue creation and deletion, so check for unnecessary temporary queues or optimize the queue lifecycle management.
表示每秒在 RabbitMQ 中新创建的 AMQP 通道数量。通道用于客户端与服: Number of new AMQP channels created in RabbitMQ per second. Channels are used for efficient communication between clients and the server, but creating too many of them too often increases resource consumption. An abnormally high value may indicate that the application abuses short-lived channels or manages connections badly, and should be optimized to avoid performance degradation or memory pressure.
表示每秒在 RabbitMQ 中新创建的队列数量。此指标反映系统处理新队列请求的: Number of queues newly created in RabbitMQ per second. This metric reflects how often new queue requests are handled; an abnormally high value may indicate that the application creates queues too frequently, so check the business logic to avoid wasting resources or degrading performance because of too many queues.
表示每秒在 RabbitMQ 中新建立的客户端连接数量。该指标反映客户端连接请求: Number of client connections newly established in RabbitMQ per second. This metric reflects how active client connection requests are. A sudden increase may be caused by frequent client reconnects (misconfiguration, network jitter) or by a traffic burst, so analyze it together with the connection close rate (Connections closed/s) to detect abnormal short-lived connections. A persistently high value increases the load on the server, so the connection reuse strategy of clients should be optimized.
表示每秒在 RabbitMQ 中被关闭的 AMQP 通道数量。通道关闭是正常通信: Number of AMQP channels closed in RabbitMQ per second. Channel closing is the result of a normal end of communication or of an abnormal interruption. If this metric does not match the channel creation rate (Channels opened/s) over a long period, it may reflect connection leaks, clients not releasing resources properly or frequent reconnects, so check the error logs for potential issues.
表示每秒在 RabbitMQ 中被关闭的客户端连接数量。连接关闭可能是正常终止（: Number of client connections closed in RabbitMQ per second. A connection may be closed normally (the client disconnects on purpose) or abnormally (network problems, heartbeat timeout). If this metric spikes or does not match the connection creation rate (Connections opened/s), clients may be reconnecting abnormally or failing to release resources, or the server may be closing connections itself (traffic limits, authentication failures, etc.), so analyze the logs to find the exact cause.
表示每秒在 RabbitMQ 中被删除的队列数量。此指标反映系统清理或回收队列资: Number of queues deleted in RabbitMQ per second. This metric reflects how often the system cleans up or reclaims queue resources. An abnormally high value may indicate that the application creates queues and deletes them right away, so check for bad resource management or abuse of temporary queues in order to avoid unnecessary performance overhead.
表示消费者在手动确认（manual acknowledgment）模式下每秒执行: Number of polling operations performed per second by consumers in manual acknowledgment mode. This metric reflects how often consumers fetch messages from queues and have to acknowledge them explicitly (ACK/NACK); abnormal values may be caused by consumer processing delays or a message backlog, so optimize the acknowledgment logic or adjust the concurrency.
表示消费者在自动确认（auto acknowledgment）模式下每秒执行的轮: Number of polling operations performed per second by consumers in auto acknowledgment mode. This metric reflects how often consumers fetch messages from queues; abnormal values may be caused by insufficient consumer capacity or network latency, so adjust the polling interval or improve consumer performance.
表示消费者在自动确认（auto acknowledgment）模式下每秒接收的消: Number of messages received per second by consumers in auto acknowledgment mode. This metric reflects the message processing capacity of the system when no manual acknowledgment is required; abnormal values may be caused by slow consumers or a message backlog, so watch consumer performance or adjust the concurrency settings.
表示消费者每秒成功接收的消息数量。此指标反映系统的实时吞吐量，若数值低于预期，可: Number of messages successfully received by consumers per second. This metric reflects the real-time throughput of the system; a value lower than expected may be caused by insufficient consumer capacity, network latency or a queue backlog, so optimize consumer performance or scale out.
表示消费者每秒成功确认（acknowledged）的消息数量。此指标反映消费者处: Number of messages successfully acknowledged by consumers per second. This metric reflects how efficiently consumers finish processing messages; a low value may be caused by business processing delays, network problems or an abnormal acknowledgment mechanism, so check consumer performance and whether the acknowledgment logic is reasonable.
表示消费者每秒执行轮询（polling）操作但未获取到任何消息的次数。此指标反映: Number of polling operations per second in which consumers got no message at all. This metric reflects how idle the queue is and how efficient consumer polling is; a high value may mean that there are not enough messages in the queue or that the polling interval is not reasonable, so adjust the polling strategy of consumers or check how producers generate messages.
表示生产者每秒向队列或交换机发布的消息数量。此指标反映系统的写入负载，突增可能影: Number of messages published by producers to queues or exchanges per second. This metric reflects the write load of the system; a sudden increase may cause a message backlog or processing delays.
表示队列中已准备好但尚未被消费者接收的消息数量。此指标反映消费者的处理能力，若持: Number of messages in queues that are ready but have not yet been received by consumers. This metric reflects the processing capacity of consumers; continuous growth may indicate that consumers are too slow or blocked.
队列中已准备好被消费但尚未被消费者获取的消息总数。建议监控阈值：>1000时警告: Total number of messages in queues that are ready to be consumed but have not yet been fetched by consumers. Suggested monitoring thresholds: warning above 1000 (consumers may not keep up), critical above 10000 (check the status of consumers). This metric is used to assess the message backlog; if it keeps growing, check the processing capacity of consumers or the network latency.
